title: Datastore Capacity Trending
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:inv:datastore"
| eval used_pct = round((capacity - freeSpace) / capacity * 100, 1)
| stats latest(used_pct) as used_pct, latest(freeSpace) as free_GB by name
| eval free_GB = round(free_GB / 1073741824, 1)
| where used_pct > 80
| sort -used_pct
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` avg(Performance.storage_used_percent) as disk_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.mount span=1h
| where disk_pct > 85
description: A full datastore prevents VM disk writes, causing crashes and corruption. Datastores fill gradually from VM growth, snapshots, and log accumulation.
value: A full datastore prevents VM disk writes, causing crashes and corruption. Datastores fill gradually from VM growth, snapshots, and log accumulation.
implementation: TA-vmware collects datastore inventory automatically. Set alerts at 80% (warning), 90% (high), 95% (critical). Use `predict` command for 30-day forecasting. Include snapshot size in the analysis.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-vmware`.
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=vmware:perf:datastore` or `sourcetype=vmware:inv:datastore`.
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
TA-vmware collects datastore inventory automatically. Set alerts at 80% (warning), 90% (high), 95% (critical). Use `predict` command for 30-day forecasting. Include snapshot size in the analysis.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:inv:datastore"
| eval used_pct = round((capacity - freeSpace) / capacity * 100, 1)
| stats latest(used_pct) as used_pct, latest(freeSpace) as free_GB by name
| eval free_GB = round(free_GB / 1073741824, 1)
| where used_pct > 80
| sort -used_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Datastore Capacity Trending** вАФ A full datastore prevents VM disk writes, causing crashes and corruption. Datastores fill gradually from VM growth, snapshots, and log accumulation.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=vmware:perf:datastore` or `sourcetype=vmware:inv:datastore`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-vmware`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:inv:datastore. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:inv:datastore". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **used_pct** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **free_GB** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Filters the current rows with `where used_pct > 80` вАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
вАҐ Orders rows with `sort` вАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` avg(Performance.storage_used_percent) as disk_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.mount span=1h
| where disk_pct > 85
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Datastore Capacity Trending** вАФ A full datastore prevents VM disk writes, causing crashes and corruption. Datastores fill gradually from VM growth, snapshots, and log accumulation.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=vmware:perf:datastore` or `sourcetype=vmware:inv:datastore`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-vmware`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Performance` вАФ enable acceleration for that model.
вАҐ Filters the current rows with `where disk_pct > 85` вАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge per datastore, Table (name, capacity, free, % used), Line chart with predict trendline.
visualization: Gauge per datastore, Table (name, capacity, free, % used), Line chart with predict trendline.
